context: David Hailey (Countifi) is the first paying pilot. After this sprint every pitch level (L1-L4) generates without errors, logos render correctly, Market Intelligence displays clean data, the prospect-to-outbound pipeline works end-to-end, and every deploy runs through CI automatically.

stories.title: Add NemoClaw HTTP route
stories.description: The service function sendProspectsToNemoClaw() exists in prospectIntelService.js (line 667) but there is no HTTP route in prospectIntelRoutes.js. David Hailey's prospect-to-outbound pipeline is broken end-to-end.

key_facts.auth_pattern: requireAuth middleware (matches all other routes in this file)
key_facts.response: NemoClaw API response or { error, message }

acceptance_criteria: POST /prospect-intel/batch/:batchId/send-to-nemoclaw added to prospectIntelRoutes.js
Route calls sendProspectsToNemoClaw(batchId, prospectIds, req.user.sub, options)
Route uses requireAuth middleware
Returns NemoClaw response or appropriate 4xx/5xx error
882+ tests still pass
guards: Do NOT modify the service function
Do NOT change other routes
Only add the route handler

stories.title: Verify and fix L4 One-Pager generation
stories.description: L4 One-Pager broke during David's live Countifi demo in March. The isL4 check in level2Generator.js requires BOTH pitchLevel === 4 AND useCustomLibrary to be true. If generateLibraryEnhancedContent() returns null, useCustomLibrary is false and isL4 is false, causing silent fallback to generic L2 template.

key_facts.case_4_added: March 19, 2026 — case 4 added to generatePitch() switch at line 576
key_facts.generateLevel4_function: functions/api/pitchGenerator.js — validatesSales Library, delegates to generateLevel2()
key_facts.useCustomLibrary_condition: options.useCustomLibrary && libraryContent — false if generateLibraryEnhancedContent() returns null
key_facts.known_silent_fallback: If generateLibraryEnhancedContent() returns null, L4 silently renders as generic L2
key_facts.guard_lines: generateLibraryEnhancedContent() has TWO guard returns (lines 72 and 95) — both must be checked

acceptance_criteria: Generate L4 pitch for David's UID against a test prospect — no errors
L4 renders with Sales Library content visible (proof points, methodology, case study blocks)
L4 PDF download works
No regressions to L1, L2, L3
882+ tests still pass
guards: Do NOT modify L1/L2/L3 handlers
Do NOT change Gemini model or prompt format
Do NOT modify credit deduction logic
If generateLibraryEnhancedContent() returns null, investigate why — do not mask the error

stories.title: Verify and fix logo rendering on L2/L3 pitches
stories.description: Logos not rendering on L2 (one-pager) and L3 (slide deck) pitches. Reported during Countifi demo. Audit found: logos stored at Firebase Storage logos/{userId}/{fileName} with public read. Logo URLs are public download URLs (not signed URLs) stored on users/{uid}.sellerProfile.branding.logo. No CORS config on GCS bucket — logos may fail in puppeteer PDF renders.

key_facts.l2_logo_field: customLogo = public URL from seller profile
key_facts.storage_rule: allow read: if true (line 10 of storage.rules)
key_facts.no_cors_config: CONFIRMED — no gcs-cors.json, no CORS in firebase.json
key_facts.puppeteer_pdf: pdfGenerator.js uses puppeteer-core — cross-origin logo loads can fail without CORS headers
key_facts.audit_risk: H2 — Firebase Storage CORS not configured for bucket

acceptance_criteria: Upload a logo in Settings
Generate L2 pitch — logo appears in rendered output
Generate L3 pitch — logo appears on title slide
Logo persists across page refreshes (not a temporary blob URL)
PDF download shows logo
882+ tests still pass
guards: Do NOT change Storage bucket configuration via gcloud — document the CORS fix needed but apply it via firebase.json or document the manual gcloud command
Do NOT modify pitch content generation logic
Do NOT change the Storage rules (public read is intentional for logos)
expected_files_changed: Likely: functions/api/pitch/level2Generator.js or templateOnePager.js (logo URL path fix)
Possibly: gcs-cors.json (new file documenting CORS config to apply)

stories.title: Fix [object Object] in Market Intelligence Timing Recommendation
stories.description: Timing Recommendation section in Market Intelligence displays '[object Object]' instead of formatted text. Frontend at market.js:4056 renders demand.recommendations.timing — if timing is itself an object rather than a string, naive template interpolation produces [object Object].

key_facts.likely_bug: demand.recommendations.timing is an object, not a string — direct template interpolation renders [object Object]
key_facts.fix_approach: Either serialize timing to a string in the backend before storing, or add typeof check + JSON.stringify in the frontend renderer

acceptance_criteria: Generate a Market Intel report for any prospect
Timing Recommendation section displays readable text, NOT '[object Object]'
No regressions to other Market Intel sections
882+ tests still pass
guards: Do NOT change Opportunity Score logic
Do NOT change demographics or share of voice rendering
Fix only the timing recommendation serialization

stories.title: Fix one-pager popup sizing on initial render
stories.description: One-pager popup does not maintain standard sizing on initial render — reported as inconsistent during Countifi demo.
read_first: synchintro-app/js/pages/ (search for popup, modal, onepager, one-pager)

acceptance_criteria: Generate L2 one-pager, click to view in popup
Popup renders at consistent, readable size on first open
No flicker or resize after initial render
882+ tests still pass
guards: Do NOT change popup content
Frontend only — no backend changes
expected_files_changed: 1 file — popup component CSS or JS

stories.title: Fix DataForSEO reviews endpoint 404
stories.description: DataForSEO /business_data/google/reviews/live/advanced has been returning 404 since May 18. Review enrichment on Market Intel reports is blocked.

investigation_steps: 1. Check DataForSEO API changelog for endpoint changes
2. Test the endpoint directly with valid credentials

acceptance_criteria: Identify whether endpoint URL changed or account is out of credits
Update endpoint URL in dataForSEOClient.js if it changed
Generate a Market Intel report and confirm reviews section populates

guards: Do NOT change other DataForSEO endpoints (SERP, Lighthouse, Maps)

stories.title: Fix processThresholdAlerts cron interval
stories.description: processThresholdAlerts runs every 5 minutes instead of every 6 hours. This is a 72x cost overrun in Cloud Functions invocations.

key_facts.cost_impact: 72x more Cloud Functions invocations than intended

acceptance_criteria: Change schedule from 'every 5 minutes' to 'every 6 hours'
882+ tests still pass
Deploy and verify in Firebase Console
guards: Do NOT change the alert logic itself
Only fix the schedule expression

stories.title: Fix overly-permissive Firestore rules
stories.description: Three collections readable by any authenticated user: pitchAnalytics, pitchVersions, teamInvitations. Five new collections have NO rules: aisynchSubscriptions, aiReadinessRateLimits, publicDataEnrichmentCache, irsBmfCache, visibilityEnrichmentCache.

overly_permissive.pitchAnalytics: allow read: if isAuthenticated() — should be userId scoped
overly_permissive.pitchVersions: allow read: if isAuthenticated() — exposes full pitch HTML cross-user
overly_permissive.teamInvitations: allow read: if isAuthenticated() — any user can read all invites

key_facts.fix_for_no_rules: allow read, write: if false — Cloud Functions use Admin SDK, bypasses rules
key_facts.pitchAnalytics_note: April 16 CLAUDE.md says 'allow read: if isAuthenticated()' was a deliberate fix for analytics permissions bug — tightening to userId requires that userId is written on the doc
key_facts.teamInvitations_fix: allow read: if isAuthenticated() && (resource.data.inviteeEmail == request.auth.token.email || resource.data.teamOwnerUid == request.auth.uid)

acceptance_criteria: pitchAnalytics: restricted to userId match OR keep if analytics docs have no userId field (document finding)
pitchVersions: restricted to resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid
teamInvitations: restricted to invitee email or team owner
5 no-rules collections: allow read, write: if false
Deploy rules successfully
882+ tests still pass
Existing pitch analytics tab still works after tightening
guards: Do NOT change rules for prospectIntel, Market Intel, or creditLedger
Verify pitchAnalytics docs have userId field before restricting — check April 16 CLAUDE.md note
Test that analytics tab still renders after rules change

stories.title: Add Census API key for full demographics

key_facts.impact: Demographics on Market Intel reports use Serper snippets instead of ACS data

acceptance_criteria: Verify CENSUS_API_KEY status in functions/.env
If missing/invalid: obtain new free key and add to .env
Confirm Market Intel reports still generate (fallbacks should work regardless)
0 code files changed
guards: Do NOT change any Census service logic
Env var only — no code changes
Not blocking S10 — Census fallbacks are confirmed working

Firestore rules deploy is manual-only (not in CI)
weekday-health-audit.yml exists with bug fixes applied May 15

All 882+ tests pass in CI

stories.title: Create .env.example with all env vars
